indicators.label: Use of clean water for meal preparation

metrics.label: % HH using clean water to prepare food
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO/UNICEF guidance on safe food preparation and WASH–nutrition linkages. Threshold reflects widespread use of unsafe water for food preparation.
https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789241594639

indicators.label: Use of fuel to cook food

metrics.label: % HH not using fuel source to prepare food
% HH with safe storage equipment
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households by reported food storage method

indicators.label: Qualitative reports of food sharing practices

metrics.label: Qualitative reports of HHs sharing food with other houseohlds or individuals, especially received assistance

indicators.label: Use of surface water in food preparation

metrics.label: Qualitative reports of surface water use for food preparation

indicators.label: Qualitative reports on main food groups consumed

metrics.label: Qualitative reports on 1-2 food groups remaining (staple cereals, pulses or else) in the area, possibly only through in-kind assistance, hot meals distribution or wild foods gathering
% HHs with significant decrease in average # of months of food stocks

indicators.label: Availability of monitored staple cereals in the market

metrics.label: # of monitored staple cereals for which limited availability or no availability is reported
% of localities where very insufficient or no food available % of localities where not enough food is available on markets
% of localities or share of pop in a locality where access to farmlandand/or pasture is not possible or extremely limited

indicators.label: Qualitative reports on inavailability of food

metrics.label: Qualitative reports that key food sources are depleted or extremely low (local crops destroyed, flooded, uncultivated OR markets without food), and/or alterative food sources are mainly providing irregular amounts of food to the area (wild foods, aid, etc.)

indicators.label: Qualitative reports on depleted food stocks

metrics.label: Qualitative reports on food stocks (local or imported crops, warehouses stocks, etc.) at area level being depleted or (rural areas) that households' stocks are depleted (most often local crops).
% HHs mainly relying on atypical food sources
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households by main sources of food in the 30 days prior to data collection
metrics.remarks_limitations:
Atypical should be defined per context. Default options include: Borrowed
Gift from family and/or friends
Begging
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on IPC and WFP coping strategy frameworks. “Atypical” food sources are context-specific and include socially or economically unsustainable sources (e.g. borrowing, begging). Threshold reflects widespread reliance on crisis coping.

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting emergency level livelihoods based coping strategies
metrics.msna_indicator: Livelihood Coping Strategy - Essential Needs (LCS-EN)

metrics.label: % change in local food prices against previous year and five-year average (incl. seasonality)
% change in ToTs for staple foods against previous year and five-year average, incl. seasonality
HEA outcome - survival deficit (households cannot meet 50% of their minimum food and non-food survival needs without irreversible coping).)
metrics.remarks_limitations: Survival deficit = inability to meet minimum food + non-food needs without irreversible coping
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on Household Economy Approach (HEA). Survival deficit indicates households cannot meet minimum food and non-food needs without irreversible coping.

metrics.label: % of HHs relying on atypical or"unstable" sources of income (exchange for labor, gifts, begging, etc.)
metrics.msna_indicator: Household income over the 30 days prior to data collection, by amount and % from each source Most common sources of household income over the 30 days prior to data collection

metrics.label: % of localities with a big increase in food prices in the last 30 days
% of localities where at least half of the pop has very limited access to food
% of localities where at least half of the pop do very unusual and desperate things just to obtain a small amount of food
% HH for whom food is 75% of expenditures
metrics.msna_indicator: Household expenditures in the 30 days prior to data collection, by amount and % per type

metrics.label: IPC AFI phase
metrics.remarks_limitations: FEWSNET phases, or Cadre Harmonisé phases can also be used as equivalent
metrics.references_for_threshold: IPC AFI Classification (Phase 4 = Emergency, Phase 5 = Castarophe/Famine)

metrics.label: % of people in CARI P4
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on WFP CARI classification; threshold adapted to reflect a substantial share of the population in severe food insecurity (Phase 4), aligned with 20% rule from the IPC Guidelines.

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting severe or very severe hunger
metrics.msna_indicator: Household Hunger Scale
metrics.remarks_limitations: Can use PHU4IMPACT functions to compute the composite scores
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold for area-level flagging, based on IPC AFI Reference table: HHS 4 corresponds with IPC AFI Phase 4 (Emergency), HHS 5-6 corresponds with IPC AFI Phase 5 (Catastrophe/Famine)

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting very severe hunger
metrics.msna_indicator: Household Hunger Scale
metrics.remarks_limitations: Can use PHU4IMPACT functions to compute the composite scores
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold for area-level flagging, based on IPC AFI Reference table: HHS 4 corresponds with IPC AFI Phase 4 (Emergency), HHS 5-6 corresponds with IPC AFI Phase 5 (Catastrophe/Famine)

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting high consumption-based coping
metrics.msna_indicator: Reduced Coping Strategies Index
metrics.remarks_limitations: Can use PHU4IMPACT functions to compute the composite scores
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold for area-level flagging. WFP typical benchmark / IPC reference table lists an rCSI over 19 as crisis/emergency conditions. Value here is reflecting high proportion of population engaging in high coping.

metrics.label: % of HHs scoring poor FCS score
metrics.msna_indicator: Food Consumption Score
metrics.remarks_limitations: Can use PHU4IMPACT functions to compute the composite scores
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold for area-level flagging. WFP Standard threshold for poor FCS is ≤21. Value of 20% aligned with "20% rule" from the IPC guidelines.

metrics.label: % of localities experiencing severe or extreme hunger
metrics.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. ANA analytical hreshold based on alignment with IPC severity logic.

metrics.label: Qualitative reports of people (reasonable share per unit of analysis) not eating for a few days (2-4 days) visible due to overall catastrophic conditions within the unit of analysis. Other context- specific indications can include: people tying a rope or piece of cloth around their waist to stop hunger pangs, extreme reliance on wild foods, or extreme reliance on social networks (incl. begging)
metrics.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. ANA analytical hreshold based on alignment with IPC severity logic.

indicators.label: Qualitative reports of mainly children eating

metrics.label: Qualitative reports indicating that adults within a household are skipping meals or reducing their food consumption to favor children's or pregnant and lactating women's food consumption
metrics.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. ANA analytical hreshold based on alignment with IPC severity logic.

metrics.label: % of HHS reporting a low dietary diversity score
metrics.msna_indicator: Household Food Consumption (HDDS)
metrics.references_for_threshold: HDDS of 0-2 food groups aligns with IPC AFI Phase 4 or 5. No universally agreed global threshold for % of HHs, this ANA analytical threshold is based on alignment with IPC severity logic, 40% indicating a widespread issue.

metrics.label: MDD-W => 5 food groups at least
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Aligned with FAO-MDD-W guidelines (standard is ≥5 of 10 food groups)
https://www.fao.org/nutrition/assessment/tools/minimum-dietary-diversity-women/en/

metrics.label: % HHs with [significant] decrease in livestock production
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.msna_indicator: How many {fsl_livestock_type} did you have at the same time last year? How many {fsl_livestock_type} do you have now?

metrics.label: % HHs planting less than previous season
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households planting less than $reference year$

metrics.label: % HHs with low harvest expectation
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households by harvest expectation

metrics.label: Agricultural Stress Index (ASI)
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: Decrease in (ha) cultivated compared to previous same season
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of localities where there has been a decrease in livestock (more than half at least)
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of localities where crop production quantity from the last harvest was much less or zero compared to previous harvest
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of households with at least two water containers (one for storage and one for collection)
metrics.references_for_threshold: No global threshold, often referenced in WASH programming guidance.

indicators.label: Cost of water containers, treatment materials, or other items related to water utilization in the market

metrics.label: % change in price of water containers
% change in water treatment materials
Water containers not available in market
Water treatment materials not available in market

indicators.label: Qual reports of struggles with water treatment

metrics.label: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious challenges with water utilization due to lack of options to treat water (drinking unsafe water directly, lack of fuel to boil water, etc.) and that it is putting them at heightened risk for water borne disease transmission

indicators.label: Qual reports of struggles with water storage

metrics.label: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious challenges with water utilization due to lack of water storage capacity (water consumption and use limited by number of containers, utilizing unclean/contaminated or broken water containers, lack of water containers in the market, etc.) and that it is putting them at heightened risk for water borne disease transmission

indicators.label: Qual reports of poor water quality

metrics.label: Indications that MOST of the population may be reliant on drinking water sources that are exposed to some contamination (e.g. sources of contamination near or at water point including human or animal feces, dead animals, trash or other solid waste, flood waters or other standing/stangant water, etc.)

indicators.label: Qual reports of populations struggling to meet water needs

metrics.label: Indications that most of the population is struggling to access their minimum water needs. (e.g. reported long queues, fighting at water points, spikes in drinking water prices, use of livelihood coping strategies to buy water)
% HHs with HWISE-4
metrics.msna_module: Drinking water - HWISE-12 Drinking water - HWISE-12 / Urban
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on HWISE scale; no globally agreed severity cut-offs exist. ANA threshold reflects widespread household water insecurity (>50%). 
https://www.rural-water-supply.net/en/resources/991-

indicators.label: Water availability at main water source

metrics.label: Average number of days per week of water availability at main water source
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households by number of days water was unavailable from main source in the past month (XW8)
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Sphere minimum standard reflects reliable daily access to water. ANA threshold here to indicate acutely severe availability gap. 
https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-Handbook-2018-EN.pdf

metrics.label: % of households with 4 days or less per week of water availability at main water source
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households by number of days water was unavailable from main source in the past month (XW8)
metrics.remarks_limitations: Question asks about the past month but can perhaps proxy from this
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Sphere minimum standard reflects reliable daily access to water. ANA threshold here to indicate significant proportion of the population affected by service gap. 
https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-Handbook-2018-EN.pdf

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting they use less preferred, unimproved or unsafe water sources to cope with lack of drinking water
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households engaging in coping mechanisms due water insufficiency, by type of coping mechanism
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA threshold, reflecting significant proportion of population using severe coping mechanism.

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting reducing water usage for hygiene to cope with lack of water
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households engaging in coping mechanisms due water insufficiency, by type of coping mechanism
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA threshold, reflecting majority of population using severe coping mechanism.

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting [other severe context specific coping] to cope with lack of water
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households engaging in coping mechanisms due water insufficiency, by type of coping mechanism
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA threshold, reflecting majority of population using severe coping mechanism.

indicators.label: Qual reports of stressed availability of water at HH level

metrics.label: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be experiencing household level stress on availability of water at the household level (inadequate storage for water preventing adequate water consumption, water service interruptions, etc.)
% of HHs with travel and queue time to water source more than 30 minutes
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households by time (minutes) taken to fetch water (round trip by walking, queuing and time needed to fetch water) (W3/W4)
metrics.references_for_threshold: Sphere minimum standard = ≤30 minutes round trip. ANA threshold suggests significant share of population unable to make this roundtrip within that timeframe. https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-Handbook-2018-EN.pdf

metrics.label: % of HHs spending more than 5% of income on water
metrics.msna_indicator: Household expenditures in the 30 days prior to data collection, by amount and % per type
metrics.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. 3-5% is often used as affordability threshold. The UN Resolution on Human Rights in Drinking Water and Sewerage (24 March 2011) sets the threshold at 5%, elsewhere 3% is reported (e.g., https://www.un.org/en/global-issues/water#:~:text=One%20of%20the%20most%20important,should%20not%20exceed%2030%20minutes.)

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting physical barriers to accessing water points
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households having had problems related to access to water, by type of problem

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting financial access barriers to accessing water points
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households having had problems related to access to water, by type of problem

indicators.label: Qual reports of extreme water coping strategies

metrics.label: Indications that MOST of the population may be resorting to extreme coping behaviours to meet their daily water needs (spending significant daily time to fetch water each day, using unsafe or contaminated water sources, displacement due to lack of water, etc.)

indicators.label: Qual reports of limited physical access to water

metrics.label: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious physical access constraints to accessing their main drinking water sources (insecurity or lack of safety on the way to or at the water point, flooding or other natural hazard cutting off access, etc.)

indicators.label: Qual reports of limited financial access to water

metrics.label: Indications that some of the popluation may be facing serious financial access constraints to accessing their main drinking water sources (extreme price increases double or triple the normal price, selling of assets to afford water, etc.)

indicators.label: Quantity of total water per capita

metrics.label: Median litres total water per person per day
metrics.remarks_limitations: Low total water = signal of system stress/collapse
metrics.references_for_threshold: Shpere minimum standard is 15L/person/day for all uses. ANA threshold reflects severe shorfall (<50% of minimum standard), consistent with emergency conditions

indicators.label: Quantity of drinking and cooking water per capita

metrics.label: Median litres drinking/cooking water per person per day
metrics.remarks_limitations:
Low drinking water indicates potential survival risk
Low domestic water indicates hygiene breakdown, potential disease risk
metrics.references_for_threshold: Sphere minimum standard is 6L/person/day for drinking + cooking. ANA threshold reflects severe shortfall consistent with emergency conditions.

indicators.label: Quantity of domestic water per capita

metrics.label: Median litres domestic water per person per day
metrics.remarks_limitations: Low domestic water indicates hygiene breakdown, potential disease risk
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on Sphere: Total water (15L) - drinking/cooking (6L) leaves 9L/person/day as the domsetic threshold.

metrics.label: % of HHs with >100 CFU/100ml at point of use (storage container, etc.)
metrics.remarks_limitations: Direct exposure risk
metrics.references_for_threshold:
WHO Drinking water quality guidelines. Risk categories: 0 CFU = safe, 1-10 CFU = low risk, 10-100 is intermediate risk, >100 is high risk of contamination. ANA analytical threshold of 40% suggests significant exposure. 
https://www.who.int/teams/environment-climate-change-and-health/water-sanitation-and-health/water-safety-and-quality/drinking-water-quality-guidelines

indicators.label: Use of unimproved drinking water sources

metrics.label: % of HHs relying on unimproved water sources for drinking/cooking water
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households having had access to an improved drinking water source % of households by type of primary source of drinking water (W1)
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on JMP service ladder (WHO/UNICEF). ANA analytical threshold indicates widespread use (>80% of HHs, systemic issue). 
https://washdata.org/topics/drinking-water

metrics.label: % of HHs relying on surface water as primary water source for drinking and cooking
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households having had access to an improved drinking water source % of households by type of primary source of drinking water (W1)
metrics.remarks_limitations: Reliance on surface water indicates high vulnerability, increased disease risk
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on JMP service ladder (WHO/UNICEF). ANA analytical threshold indicates widespread use (>20% of HHs, significant share of HHs). 
https://washdata.org/topics/drinking-water

metrics.label: % of assesssed settlements reported surface water as the most common source of water for drinking and cooking for people in the community
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

indicators.label: Sufficient number of functional water points

metrics.label: # of people per tap stand
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: # of people per water pump
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: Average # of hours spent queing for water collection per trip
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

indicators.label: Availability of clean drinking and cooking water in the community

metrics.label: % of assessed settlements reporting there is a very serious or extremely serious problem AND that most people or everyone has limited access to drinking/cooking water
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % change in drinking water price in the last month (or previous baseline)
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of assessed settlements reporting large water price increases in the last 30 days
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: Qualitative reports of exclusion of certain groups from programme staff
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of assessed settlements reporting social barriers to accessing drinking and cooking water
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of assessed settlements reporting most people need more than 30min to get to the main water source, collect water, and return
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

indicators.label: Hygienic conditions and quality of sanitation faclities

metrics.label: % of HH with access to unimproved sanitation facilities
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households with access to improved sanitation facilities s% of households using a sanitation facility, by type of sanitation facility used (S1)
metrics.remarks_limitations: Check contextualized options for unimproved facilities

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting hygiene related issues with latrines
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households having had problems related to sanitation facility access, by type of problem

metrics.label: % of HHs reporting protection or safety related issues with latrine
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households having had problems related to sanitation facility access, by type of problem

metrics.label: % of latrines that meet minimum technical requirements
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of latrines that are hygienic and cleaned on a regular basis
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of latrines meeting minimum protection requirements (locks, sex seperated, etc.)
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: Qualitative reports of exclusion of certain groups
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of assessed settlements reporting there is a very serious or extremely serious problem with safe latrine access, reporting physical access as a main reason people are not using latrines
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of assessed settlements reporting there is a very serious or extremely serious problem with safe latrine access, reporting social access as a main reason people are not using latrines
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of people sleeping under an insecticide treated bed net the previous night
% change in cost of mosquito nets
No mosquito nets available in market

indicators.label: Qualitative reports of exposure to disease vectors

metrics.label: Indications that some of the population may have increased exposure to disease vectors due to inadequate shelter or the environment. This may include: (a) standing water as mosquito breeding grounds (b) poor ventilation of shelters(c) contamined or waste water near shelter(d) rats, pests, or other disease carriers

Indications that MOST of the population may be overcrowded and may be facing increased risks for respiratory disease, protection concerns, or mental health distress. Some indications may include:(a) multiple households sharing shelter (b) people cramped into small shared sleeping spaces(c) not all household members can lie down or fit in the sleeping space(d) reports of widespread coughing or respiratory issues (e) reports of tensions or fighting between neighbours due to proximity and lack of privacy (f) long lines or waits for communal facilities such as latrines, water, etc.
Average covered living space per person
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on Sphere minimum standards: Minimum 3.5 m2 of living space per person, excluding cooking space, bathing area and sanitation facility. https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-in-Practice.pdf

metrics.label: % of HHs with at least 3.5 m2 of covered living space per person
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on Sphere minimum standards: Minimum 3.5 m2 of living space per person, ANA threshold suggests significant population facing gap.

metrics.label: % of HHs with inadequate shelter
metrics.msna_indicator: % of households by type of shelter they currently live in
metrics.remarks_limitations: Check contextualized options for inadequate shelter type
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on Sphere shelter standards (shelter must provide protection from climate, safety, and privacy). Threshold reflects a substantial share of households lacking adequate protection from environmental conditions; definition of inadequacy is context-specific.

indicators.label: Cost of tarpaulin or other key shelter materials

metrics.label: % change in tarpaulin or other shelter materials
No tarpaulin (or other key shelter material) available in market

Indications that MOST of the population may be living in inadequate shelter that either (a) is not durable or sturdy (b) does not provide adequate privacy (c) does not provide safety or protection from the weather/elements (d) does not provide safe place to sleep

indicators.label: Quality of SAM Nutrition Services- CMAM performance: (Cure rates, Defaulter rates, Death rates)

metrics.label: CMAM performance - cure rates-SAM %
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on SQUEAC/SLEAC (Action Against Hunger / Valid International) coverage standards, and Nutrition Working group: Technical Issue Paper #1 CMAM - specific programme indicators: >75% Cure rate. ANA thresholds reflect insufficient coverage to meet population needs. https://ec.europa.eu/echo/files/policies/sectoral/TIP%20CMAM.pdf

metrics.label: CMAM performance - death rates-SAM %
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: Based on SQUEAC/SLEAC (Action Against Hunger / Valid International) coverage standards, and Nutrition Working group: Technical Issue Paper #1 CMAM - specific programme indicator: >10% death rate. ANA thresholds reflect insufficient coverage to meet population needs.  https://ec.europa.eu/echo/files/policies/sectoral/TIP%20CMAM.pdf

metrics.label: CMAM performance - defaulter rates- SAM %
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: ased on SQUEAC/SLEAC (Action Against Hunger / Valid International) coverage standards, and Nutrition Working group: Technical Issue Paper #1 CMAM - specific programme indicators: >15% defaulters. ANA thresholds reflect insufficient coverage to meet population needs. https://ec.europa.eu/echo/files/policies/sectoral/TIP%20CMAM.pdf

indicators.label: Quality of MAM Nutrition Services- CMAM performance: (Cure rates, Defaulter rates, Death rates)

metrics.label: CMAM performance - cure rates-MAM%
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: Less standardised for MAM compared to SAM, threshold reflects similar expectations (see above)

metrics.label: CMAM performance - death rates-MAM %
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: Less standardised for MAM compared to SAM, threshold reflects lower likelihood of death  among MAM cases compared to SAM cases (see above)

metrics.label: CMAM performance - defaulter rates- MAM %
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: Less standardised for MAM compared to SAM, threshold reflects similar expectations (see above)

indicators.label: Accessibility & Availability (Coverage of SC, OTP or TSFP nutrition services.)

metrics.label: Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in IDP camps
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Sphere minimum standards for perforamnce indicators of coverage: in camp settings suggests >90% coverage as acceptable access. ANA threshold reflects severe access gap. 
https://www.indikit.net/document/17-standardised-indicators-and-categories-for-better-cmam-reporting#:~:text=This%20paper%20outlines%20standardised%20reporting,systems%20to%20fit%20best%20practice.

metrics.label: Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in urban populations
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: Sphere minimum standards for perforamnce indicators of coverage: in camp settings suggests >70% coverage as acceptable access in urban settings. ANA threshold reflects severe access gap. https://www.indikit.net/document/17-standardised-indicators-and-categories-for-better-cmam-reporting#:~:text=This%20paper%20outlines%20standardised%20reporting,systems%20to%20fit%20best%20practice.

metrics.label: Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in rural populations
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: Sphere minimum standards for perforamnce indicators of coverage: in camp settings suggests >50% coverage as acceptable access in rural settings. ANA threshold reflects severe access gap. https://www.indikit.net/document/17-standardised-indicators-and-categories-for-better-cmam-reporting#:~:text=This%20paper%20outlines%20standardised%20reporting,systems%20to%20fit%20best%20practice.

indicators.label: Access barriers accessing nutrition services in locality/catchment areas.

metrics.label: % people in the neighbourhood/locality currently facing any barriers in accessing nutrition services due to distance
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

indicators.label: Availability barriers accessing nutrition services in locality/ catchment areas

metrics.label: % people in the neighbourhood/locality currently facing any barriers in due to unavailability of nutrition facilities
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

indicators.label: Coverage of nutrition services for targeted population/ catchment areas

metrics.label: % target population in the catchment area covered by the nutrition facility
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % Measles case fatality rates
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Current estimates used by WHO for CFRs in endemic countries range between 0.05-6%. ANA threshold is set at 5% as a signal of concern. 
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19188207/

metrics.label: % Cholera case fatality rates
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: WHO handles an emergency threshold of >1% CFR for Cholera in treatment centres. According to the Global Taskforce on Cholera Control, a CFR >1% is usually due to one or a combination of different factors, such as poor access to health facilities, lack of healthcare-seeking behaviour, and/or inadequate case management. https://www.gtfcc.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/gtfcc-cholera-outbreak-response-field-manual-2024.pdf

metrics.label: % Ebola case fatality rates
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Ebola has a high baseline CFR, estimations of the Ebola CFR from the years 1976-2022 approach around 60% globally. ANA threshold set at the higher end of baseline estimates. 
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1876034123003581

metrics.label: % of health facilities providing minimum package of primary health services as defined by in country policies
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO essential health service frameworks. Threshold reflects reduced availability of core health services.
https://www.who.int/data/gho/indicator-metadata-registry/imr-details/7793#:~:text=IV.&text=Associated%20terms:,and%20the%20most%20disadvantaged%20population.&text=Method%20of%20estimation:,to%20100%20(optimal%20value).&text=Step%203:%20Calculate%20the%20geometric,values%20calculated%20in%20Step%202.

metrics.label: % of health facilties providing basic reproductive health care services (e.g.safe delivery services , emergency obstretic and newborn care, etc.)
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO essential health service frameworks. Threshold reflects reduced availability of core health services.
https://www.who.int/data/gho/indicator-metadata-registry/imr-details/7793#:~:text=IV.&text=Associated%20terms:,and%20the%20most%20disadvantaged%20population.&text=Method%20of%20estimation:,to%20100%20(optimal%20value).&text=Step%203:%20Calculate%20the%20geometric,values%20calculated%20in%20Step%202.

metrics.label: % of health facilities providing basic outpatient services
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO essential health service frameworks. Threshold reflects reduced availability of core health services.
https://www.who.int/data/gho/indicator-metadata-registry/imr-details/7793#:~:text=IV.&text=Associated%20terms:,and%20the%20most%20disadvantaged%20population.&text=Method%20of%20estimation:,to%20100%20(optimal%20value).&text=Step%203:%20Calculate%20the%20geometric,values%20calculated%20in%20Step%202.

metrics.label: % of health facilities providing basic maternal and newborn health services
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO essential health service frameworks. Threshold reflects reduced availability of core health services.
https://www.who.int/data/gho/indicator-metadata-registry/imr-details/7793#:~:text=IV.&text=Associated%20terms:,and%20the%20most%20disadvantaged%20population.&text=Method%20of%20estimation:,to%20100%20(optimal%20value).&text=Step%203:%20Calculate%20the%20geometric,values%20calculated%20in%20Step%202.

metrics.label: Number of people per primary health facility catchment area
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.remarks_limitations: Right to health imposes four essential standards on healthcare services: Availabiltiy, Accessibility, Acceptability, and Quality (AAAQ), from WHO. Indicators used here reflect this framework. https://www.who.int/news-room/questions-and-answers/item/q-a-on-the-health-workforce-crisis#:~:text=What%20do%20we%20mean%20by,outcomes%20will%20not%20be%20satisfactory.
metrics.references_for_threshold:
WHO and national planning norms often benchmark primary care facility catchment as 10,000 - 20,000 per PHC. UNHCR Emergency Handbook describes 10,000 people per basic health unit in the emergency phase. 
https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240044210 
https://emergency.unhcr.org/emergency-assistance/health-and-nutrition/primary-health-care/primary-health-care-coverage-standard

metrics.label: Number of people per secondary health facility (hospitals)
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: Number of inpatient beds per 10,000 people
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Sphere handbook specifies a minimum of 18 beds / 10,0000 persons (excluding maternity beds) to ensure adequate coverage during emergencies. ANA threshold indicates severe gap. 
http://www.humanitarianstandardspartnership.org/ViewContent?DocID=1000011&VersionID=2000017&ChapterNumber=8&OrderInChapter=6&Lang=en

metrics.label: % of functional primary health care (PHC) facilities
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: No gblobal thresholds exist, "functionality* states can be based on WHO HeRAMS or other health facility monitoring. ANA threshold reflects functionality levels consistent with major health system disruption.

metrics.label: % of functional secondary health care (SHC) facilities
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold: No gblobal thresholds exist, "functionality* states can be based on WHO HeRAMS or other health facility monitoring. ANA threshold reflects functionality levels consistent with major health system disruption.

metrics.label: Average number of days essential medicines are not available out of the past 30 days
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of health facilities with essential medicines in stock
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % of facilities reporting lack of supplies as a main functionality constraint
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: Number of skilled birth attendants per 10000 people
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO and UNICEF frameworks for skilled birth attendance and maternal health workforce capacity. No global density thresholds exist; ANA thresholds reflect severe constraints in availability of skilled maternal health personnel.
 https://data.unicef.org/indicator-profiles/MNCH_SAB/

metrics.label: Number of health workers per 10000 people
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO health workforce density benchmarks (44.5 per 10,000 for universal health coverage). ANA threshold reflects severe shortages relative to global standards.
https://www.who.int/data/gho/indicator-metadata-registry/imr-details/health-workers-density-(per-10-000-population)--by-occupation#:~:text=The%20SDG%20agenda%20gives%20recognition,Resolution%20(WHA69.

metrics.label: % of facilities reporting lack of staffing as a main functionality constraint
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors

metrics.label: % HH reporting access to a health facility in less than one hour
metrics.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metrics.msna_indicator: Average walking time to travel to nearest functioning health facility

indicators.label: Overall infectious disease incidence (all cause and all age)

metrics.label: # of consulations/treatments for infectious diseases per 1000 people per epiweek

indicators.label: Any unmet health needs in past three months

metrics.label: % of individuals with unmet health needs in the past 3 months
metrics.msna_indicator: % of individuals with a healthcare need

metrics.label: # of confirmed or suspected cases of measles in children 6 months to 15 years of age (or context specific age groups), where at least one is confirmed
metrics.references_for_threshold: WHO defines suspected measles outbreak as five or more epidemiologically linked cases, and confirmed outbreak as two or more lab-confirmed linked cases. This threshold is adapted for the ANA to flag concern.  https://www.who.int/emergencies/outbreak-toolkit/disease-outbreak-toolboxes/measles-outbreak-toolbox

metrics.label: # of cases of measles in children 6 months to 15 years of age per 1000 children, per epiweek
metrics.remarks_limitations: At this level, measles transmission is likely widespread, with high exposure among susceptible children and increased risk of complications and excess mortality in contexts with malnutrition or weak health systems.
metrics.references_for_threshold:
ANA analytical threshold in the absence of an overall global standard threshold. Based on the Sphere Handbook, WHO outbreak response guidelines, MSF field thresholds 
https://medicalguidelines.msf.org/sites/default/files/pdf/guideline-498-en.pdf

indicators.label: Reports of measles outbreaks from disease surveillance systems, rapid assessments, or other secondary sources

metrics.label: Confirmed measles outbreak, not controlled (cases increasing weekly, see epidemic curve)
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold, binary threshold is analytical rather than global severity cut-off. See WHO disease-specific toolboxes https://www.who.int/emergencies/outbreak-toolkit/disease-outbreak-toolboxes

metrics.label: # of cases of cholera/suspect cholera in populations where at least one case is confirmed
metrics.references_for_threshold: WHO/GTFCC define a suspected cholera outbreak when two or more suspected cholera cases or one suspected case with positive RDT result are reported within one surveillance unit within 7 days. This threshold is adapted for the ANA to flag outbreak severity. https://www.gtfcc.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/gtfcc-cholera-outbreak-response-field-manual-2024.pdf

metrics.label: # of cases of cholera/suspect cholera per 1000 people per week (all ages)
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold in the absence of an overall global standard threshold. Based on the Sphere Handbook, WHO outbreak response guidelines, MSF field thresholds

indicators.label: Reports of cholera outbreaks from disease surveillance systems, rapid assessments, or other secondary sources

metrics.label: Confirmed outbreak, not controlled (cases increasing weekly, see epidemic curve)

metrics.label: Reports of large increases in malaria, diarrhoeal disease, respiratory infectionsor other infectious disease not attributable to pre-crisis conditions or seasonality

metrics.label: Qualitative reports of an increase in health needs in the community (e.g. overcrowded health facilities, community events or normal activities of the community disrupted due to illnesses, increased number of deaths in the community, etc.)

% of children under-5 years with any illness in the two weeks prior to data collection
metrics.msna_indicator: % of children under-5 years of age sick in the last two weeks as reported by primary caregiver
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold, to be interpreted against seasonality, baseline incidence, surveillance quality, and triangulated evidence of service strain or outbreak escalation.

metrics.label: % of children under-5 years with any acute respiratory infection in the two weeks prior to data collection
metrics.msna_indicator: % of children under-5 years of age sick in the last two weeks as reported by primary caregiver, by symptom
metrics.remarks_limitations: Not confirmed but can obtain symptomology
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold, to be interpreted against seasonality, baseline incidence, surveillance quality, and triangulated evidence of service strain or outbreak escalation.

metrics.label: % of children under-5 years with any acute watery diarrhoea in the two weeks prior to data collection
metrics.msna_indicator: % of children under-5 years of age sick in the last two weeks as reported by primary caregiver, by symptom
metrics.remarks_limitations: Not confirmed but can obtain symptomology
metrics.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold, to be interpreted against seasonality, baseline incidence, surveillance quality, and triangulated evidence of service strain or outbreak escalation.

metrics.label: % of children 6-59 months GAM by WHZ
metrics.remarks_limitations: If data covers all chidlren 0-59 months, value would slightly over-estimate acute malnutrition, as children 0-6 months are usually be breastfed and are likely to be flagged as acuteley malnourished by WHO standards. Extra caution is advised.
metrics.references_for_threshold: Recommended threshold according to IPC Acute Malnutrition (2018). GAM 15-29.9% refers to IPC AMN Critical phase (4).

metrics.label: % of children 6-59 months GAM by MUAC
metrics.remarks_limitations: Prevalence of GAM by MUAC should be referred to as “proxy” GAM, as it is not a true representative indicator of malnutrition in the population. This is because MUAC measurements are more sensitive younger children (under 2 years of age) and will likely not detect older children that are malnourished by the WHZ criteria.
metrics.references_for_threshold: IPC Guidance suggests usign MUAC only when WHZ is not available. 10-15% GAM by MUAC corresponds roughly to IPC AMN Phase 3 and 4. Acute Needs threshold is therefore slightly conservative.

metrics.label: % of children 6-59 months combined GAM
% change of monthly new SAM admissions at Stabilization Center or OTP programs
% change of monthly defaulters to SC or OTP programs
% change of monthly deaths reported in Stabilization Centers or OTP
